# crowci_user_access_token (Resource)

Access token for authentication using API to Crow server.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "crowci_user_access_token" "ci_bot" {
  name = "ci-bot"
  scopes = [
    "admin:read",
    "admin:write",
    "repo:read",
    "repo:write",
    "repo:admin",
    "user:read",
    "user:write"
  ]
}

# With optional expiry and repo scope
resource "crowci_user_access_token" "repo_scoped" {
  name       = "repo-deploy"
  scopes     = ["repo:admin"]
  repo_id    = 42
  expires_at = 1893456000
}

output "ci_bot_token" {
  value     = crowci_user_access_token.ci_bot.token
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Token name.
- `scopes` (List of String) Token scopes.

### Optional

- `expires_at` (Number) Optional expiry as a Unix timestamp.
- `org_id` (Number) Optional org scope.
- `repo_id` (Number) Optional repo scope.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (Number) Creation time as Unix timestamp.
- `id` (Number) Token ID assigned by Crow CI.
- `last_used` (Number) Last use time as Unix timestamp.
- `token` (String, Sensitive) The token secret. Only populated on creation; not retrievable afterwards.
- `updated_at` (Number) Last update time as Unix timestamp.
- `user_id` (Number) ID of the user who owns this token.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = crowci_user_access_token.example
  id = "42"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Access token can be imported via id
terraform import crowci_user_access_token.example 42
```
